I heard a distant siren and wondered if it was a storm warning at one of the parks, or the flood warning siren from Old Ellicott City. I looked to see if there was a website notice. Instead, I found this FEMA page that looks specifically written for magat morons: 

www.fema.gov/emergency-ma...

external.description: The Integrated Public Alert and Warning System (IPAWS) is FEMA's national system for local alerting that provides authenticated emergency and life-saving information to the public through mobile phones using Wireless Emergency Alerts (WEA), to radio and television via the Emergency Alert System (EAS), on the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's NOAA Weather Radio, and to digital devices and services via the IPAWS All-Hazards Information Feed.
external.title: IPAWS Myths vs. Facts

text:
Northern, eastern Spain face floods

Aemet warned Wednesday that a cold "Santos de hielo" spell would bring heavy rain, possible Pyrenees snow and flood-risk downpours across northern and eastern Spain.

With posts by 🎓Garry Peterson, 🎓Benjamin I. Cook, 🎓Peter Thorne +8 more scholars.

What’s it going to take for City Hall to finally upzone Floyd Bennett Field for new homes?

text: Doug the Tank, a 33-pound, 30(ish)-year-old common snapping turtle was released into a suitable habitat on May 10, 2026. He was rescued in April from a Lake Michigan beach in Grand Haven, MI., after flooding likely washed him out of his native habitat. (MI Amphibian & Reptile Sanctuary) #antsat

text:
Why are local city subreddits full of the most ridiculous developer bootlickers imaginable?

I hate NIMBYs too. Yet I’m studying conservation biology as a UG and seeing people advocate for destroying more wetlands is concerning.

Don’t build housing on lands that are prone to flooding. Simple as.

images.alt: That one meme of the neckbeard wielding the knives that says “LEAVE MY [X] ALONE. But instead is has been edited to say: “Leave the greedy land developers alone” because people have been brainwashed into supporting the destruction of ecologically sensitive and important habitats.

text:
🚨 Flood Warning issued May 13 at 9:59PM EDT until May 16 at 2:00AM EDT by NWS Indianapolis IN 🚨
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
...The Flood Warning continues for the following river in Indiana...

White River at Petersburg and Hazleton.

.Heavy rainfall through the first half of last week produced minor
flooding on the lower White River. Flooding is expected to conclude
at Petersburg by Thursday afternoon and at Hazleton by Friday
evening. Dry or light spotty rainfall through Friday night will give
a pause to flooding issues for at least that long across central and
southern Indiana.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is forecast.

* WHERE...White River at Hazleton.

* WHEN...Until early Saturday morning.

* IMPACTS...At 19.0 feet, Flood waters cover the Hazleton softball
field. Pottsville Road is underwater and impassable.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- There is no current observed data.
- Forecast...The river is expected to fall below flood stage
early Friday afternoon and continue falling and remain below
flood stage.
- Flood stage is 16.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

...The Flood Warning continues for the following river in Indiana...

White River at Petersburg and Hazleton.

.Heavy rainfall through the first half of last week produced minor
flooding on the lower White River. Flooding is expected to conclude
at Petersburg by Thursday afternoon and at Hazleton by Friday
evening. Dry or light spotty rainfall through Friday night will give
a pause to flooding issues for at least that long across central and
southern Indiana.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and minor flooding is forecast.

* WHERE...White River at Petersburg.

* WHEN...Until tomorrow evening.

* IMPACTS...At 17.0 feet, Flooding affects low agricultural lands,
low oil fields and closes Bottoms Rd...Brothers Rd and East
Governors Road in Knox County.  In agricultural season...farmers
are concerned at this stage.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 9:30 PM EDT Wednesday /8:30 PM CDT Wednesday/ the stage
was 17.2 feet.
- Recent Activity...The maximum river stage in the 24 hours
ending at 9:30 PM EDT Wednesday /8:30 PM CDT Wednesday/ was
19.0 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to fall below flood stage
tomorrow morning and continue falling and remain below flood
stage.
- Flood stage is 16.0 feet.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

text: Flooding concerns are surfacing near the Perkins Road overpass, with both businesses and residents saying it's a new problem connected to a nearby construction project. Click the link for more from 2 On Your Side.

external.description: On Tuesday, WBRZ reported that some businesses on Kalurah Street near the Perkins Road overpass had flooded several times recently during rain events. It's something they haven't experienced before and question drainage along the I-10 widening project.
external.title: 'Keeps getting worse:' Flooding around interstate construction concerns nearby businesses, residents

author.displayName: Alerts from NWS St Louis MO (UNOFFICIAL)

images.alt:
...The Flood Warning continues for the following rivers in
Illinois...

Kaskaskia River at Carlyle.

River forecasts are based on observed precipitation and forecast
precipitation for the next 24 hours.

* WHAT...Minor flooding is occurring and minor flooding is forecast.

* WHERE...Kaskaskia River at Carlyle.

* WHEN...Until Tuesday afternoon.

* IMPACTS...At 20.3 feet, Township roads downstream in Covington
begin flooding near this height.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 8:00 PM CDT Wednesday the stage was 20.3 feet.
- Recent Activity...The maximum river stage in the 24 hours ending
at 8:00 PM CDT Wednesday was 20.3 feet.
- Forecast...The river is expected to rise to a crest of 20.3 feet
just after midnight tonight. It will then fall below flood stage
late Monday morning.
- Flood stage is 16.5 feet.

text:
San Diego is gorgeous and definitely does have very boring weather - coming from someone who loves storm watching and finds tornados fascinating. 😂
But at least it doesn't come with the other weather hazards; heat waves, fire, and occasional flooding near the rivers.

text:
As #Virginia experiences growing #flood losses bc of #climatechange, the brunt will be felt in the Southwest of the state, along with Chesapeake Bay & Norfolk. Yet Southwest Virginia lacks key #weather data. What are cost-savvy solutions?
www.wvtf.org/news/2026-05...

images.alt:
Headline on Radio IQ NPR story: Weather data inequity in a flash flood hotspot

Intro: July 18th was a sunny summer day in Russell County in Southwest Virginia -- until a thunderstorm cropped up over the small former coal town of Dante. No one thought much of it. Afternoon storms are common in July...

Photo of a white woman with sandy hair pulled back in a bun standing in front of a small vinyl sided home. 
Caption: Tina Sanders, 50, and her boyfriend were riding in a truck last July (2025) when their road, Sawmill Hollow, was suddenly underwater during flash flooding. Sanders was pulled under a tunnel, along Sawmill Hollow, down Lick Creek where she hit her head and the strong waters dragged her down through the town of Dante. She said it's a miracle she survived.

record.bridgyOriginalText:
#NWS #flood #nwsflashflood #FlashFloodWarning Flash Flood Warning for Brooks, GA; Thomas, GA #GAwx
At 1253 AM EDT, Doppler radar indicated thunderstorms producing
heavy rain across the warned area. Between 5 and 9 inches of rain
have fallen. Additional rainfall amounts up to 0.5 inches are
possible in the warned area. Flash flooding is ongoing or expected
to begin shortly.

HAZARD...Flash flooding caused by thunderstorms.

SOURCE...Radar.

IMPACT...Flash flooding of small creeks and streams, urban areas,
highways, streets and underpasses as well as other poor
drainage and low-lying areas.

Some locations that will experience flash flooding include...
Pavo, Barwick and Hollis.

text: A character diving into a flooded river? It must be a book about 4 young magic users, doing elemental magic in the city of York, learning to use their magic through different eras of the city's history, while fending off racist bullies, and also they deal with mental health issues in one family.

text:
Flood Advisory issued May 13 at 4:04PM AKDT until May 14 at 3:45PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam continues.

* WHERE...Bethel, Kwethluk

* WHEN...Until 345 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
This includes Kwethluk as well as low-lying areas in Bethel
including near Brown Slough and Alligator Acres. River or stream
flows are elevated.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 404 PM AKDT, The River Watch Team reported water levels
near 8.6 feet in Bethel at the BETA2 river gauge. These
levels should remain steady or slowly rise through tomorrow.
An ice jam remains in place a few miles south of Bethel,
between Napakiak and Oscarville-Napaskiak. Impounding water
due to the ice jam will cause river rises.
- http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

- If you live near or along the Kuskokwim Rover at Bethel,
including Brown Slough and Alligator Acres, or Kwethluk stay alert
and be prepared to take action.

text:
Flood Advisory issued May 13 at 4:05PM AKDT until May 14 at 4:00PM AKDT by NWS Anchorage AK
Additional Details Here.

images.alt:
* WHAT...Flooding caused by an ice jam is expected.

* WHERE...Tuluksak

* WHEN...Until 400 PM AKDT Thursday.

* IMPACTS...Minor flooding in low-lying and poor drainage areas.
River or stream flows are elevated.

* ADDITIONAL DETAILS...
- At 404 PM AKDT, The River Watch Team reported that the ice
jam in the channels of the Kuskokwim River immediately
adjacent to Tuluksak remains in place.  Water levels remain
high around town and will likely remain steady or slowly
rise. Impounding water due to the ice jam is causing water to
rise.


- Http://www.weather.gov/safety/flood

- If you live near or along Tuluksak stay alert and be prepared to
take action.
